$ASTS is having a rough day after Blue Origin’s New Glenn exploded during a static fire test at LC-36.

Important to call out that no AST SpaceMobile satellite was on the rocket since this was a test firing with no payload. So the near-term launch catalyst hasn't changed with BB8, BB9 and BB10 still expected to launch in mid-June on a SpaceX Falcon 9.

The issue is the 2026 deployment schedule since AST needs multiple launch providers to hit its target of 45 satellites in orbit by the end of 2026 and New Glenn was expected to be a key part of that launch cadence. 

Blue Origin was already dealing with an FAA investigation into the April second-stage failure which had introduced some uncertainty into this year’s launch plan but last night’s explosion adds a much larger problem because LC-36 is currently Blue Origin’s only operational New Glenn launch pad.

If the pad suffered significant damage (probably did) New Glenn’s return-to-service timeline will stretch meaningfully making it harder for AST to hit its 2026 satellite deployment target under the current launch plan.

Thats why the stock is selling off since AST can pursue additional launch providers over time but new launch diversification does not fully solve a near-term 2026 timing issue.

$ONDS secured over $30M in new May orders bringing Q2 orders-to-date above $110M across defense and autonomous systems.

They now see 19% to 20% supply-demand gap in 2H26 with shortages likely extending through 2027 & 2028 benefiting suppliers $MU, Samsung & SK Hynix.

Publishers have been losing traffic to AI overviews and zero-click search for two years.

Our data shows that readers who ask questions on publisher sites generate 3x more revenue and engage 2 to 3x more with content than passive visitors.

Dan Nathan asked @AdamSingolda if DeeperDive makes up for the traffic loss. 

His answer: it doesn’t just make up for it. It grows the pie entirely.

Full conversation on the @RiskReversal Media YouTube channel.

The consensus thinks the blockade is forcing them to make this deal. I disagree

ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet told Reuters that he sees the possibility for sporadic bottlenecks to emerge throughout the supply chain of the chip market for the foreseeable future.

$ASML $NVDA $TSM $AMD

Anthropic also announced that it reached a $47 billion run rate earlier this month, up from $30 billion in early April and $14 billion in February.

$NVDA $MSFT $AMZN $GOOG

Anthropic has raised $65 billion in funding at a $965 billion valuation, surpassing OpenAI's most recent $852 billion valuation. 

Anthropic says the funding will help expand capacity to meet growing demand for Claude. 

$NVDA $MSFT $AMZN $GOOG

UBS estimates the server CPU TAM to scale from $31 billion in 2025 to $170 billion by 2030, with a majority of this growth driven by AI CPUs, rising from a $7 billion TAM last year to $125 billion by 2030.

$NVDA $AMD $ARM $INTC

Oracle is reportedly planning to deploy hundreds of thousands of Nvidia Vera CPUs in 2026.

SpaceX’s IPO S-1 just dropped and one thing is now crystal clear:

Starlink is no longer just a side business.

It’s the economic engine powering SpaceX.

2023: ~$4B in revenue 
2025: $11B+ in revenue
And the biggest surprise is not just growth.

It’s margins.

Launch still matters, but the filing confirms what many investors suspected for a while:

Rockets may have built SpaceX. Starlink is scaling it.
